title: Cozy Citrusy Mexican Coconut Quesadilla
description: A light and cozy Mexican dish featuring the combination of Thai chili jam, coconut cream, and citrusy flavors. Perfect for a Jain-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Coconut cream
ingredients.notes: full-fat coconut cream is essential for the crack to form properly

ingredients.name: Whole wheat tortillas
ingredients.notes: any other type of tortilla will not provide the same texture

ingredients.name: Strained yogurt
ingredients.notes: plain, full-fat yogurt is necessary for the tanginess

ingredients.name: Thai chili jam
ingredients.notes: adjust to desired level of spiciness

ingredients.name: Freshly squeezed orange juice
ingredients.notes: grapefruit or lemon juice can also be used, but orange is preferred

ingredients.name: Shredded coconut
ingredients.notes: dried coconut can be used as a substitute, but it will not provide the same texture

ingredients.name: Cilantro leaves
ingredients.notes: optional, but adds a pop of freshness

instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the tortillas to the skillet and cook for 30 seconds on each side.
In a separate bowl, mix together the coconut cream, strained yogurt, Thai chili jam, and orange juice.
Place 1/4 cup of the coconut cream mixture onto half of the cooked tortilla, leaving a small border around the edges.
Sprinkle shredded coconut and cilantro leaves on top of the cream mixture, if using.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the tortilla is crispy and the filling is heated through.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Cut into wedges and serve immediately.
